name: Molino de las Pisadas y Moricacho de la Hoz — circular desde la cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calomarde offers a diverse landscape within the Montes Universales region. The area is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring river valleys, dense pine forests, and elevated plateaus. Mountain bike trails in Calomarde often include varied surfaces, from forest tracks to more technical singletrack, providing a range of challenges for riders. Best mountain bike trails around Calomarde The most popular mountain bike trail is Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural beauty of the Montes Universales. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Molino de las Pisadas y Moricacho de la Hoz — circular desde la cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro, a moderate 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route features scenic river sections and passes by historical mills. Local mountain bikers also love the Views of Albarracín – Orihuela del Tremedal loop from Calomarde, a 67.4 miles (108.4 km) trail leading through expansive landscapes with panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 9 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around Calomarde is defined by the Montes Universales, river valleys, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with some longer, more challenging routes available. The routes in Calomarde are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calomarde's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Calomarde?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails documented on komoot for the Calomarde area, offering a range of experiences within the Montes Universales region.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Calomarde?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calomarde primarily cater to moderate to difficult skill levels. There are 14 moderate routes and 7 difficult routes, with no easy trails listed, indicating a focus on more experienced riders.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Calomarde?
A: Yes, Calomarde offers several longer routes for endurance riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Albarracín – Orihuela del Tremedal loop from Calomarde&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 67.4-mile (108.4 km) trail, typically taking nearly 10 hours to complete. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moscardón – Fuente del Buey loop from Calomarde&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 48.7 miles (78.4 km).

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Calomarde?
A: The terrain around Calomarde is diverse, featuring mountainous landscapes, river valleys, and dense pine forests within the Montes Universales. Trails often combine forest tracks with more technical singletrack sections, providing varied surfaces and challenges.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and pass by natural attractions. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moricacho of the Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4542097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Mingo Marco&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008315888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de las Pisadas y Moricacho de la Hoz — circular desde la cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, features scenic river sections and historical mills.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Calomarde?
A: Yes, many routes in Calomarde are designed as circular loops. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008304509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 19.3-mile (31.0 km) loop. For a more challenging circular ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17509350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal – Bronchales Town Center loop from Torres de Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Calomarde?
A: The mountain bike trails in Calomarde are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to pine forests, and the range of challenges offered by the varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calomarde?
A: Calomarde, located in the Montes Universales, experiences cold winters with snow, making spring, summer, and early autumn the most favorable seasons for mountain biking. During these months, the weather is generally milder and trails are more accessible.

Q: Are there any specific highlights or landmarks to look out for in the Calomarde area?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, the region boasts several natural highlights. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3620455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the River Tagus&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its gorges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3826698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport an option for accessing mountain bike trails in Calomarde?
A: Calomarde is a small village in a mountainous region, and public transport options are generally limited. It is advisable to plan your trip with a private vehicle for convenient access to trailheads and to explore the wider Montes Universales area.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calomarde?
A: The trails around Calomarde are predominantly rated as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes listed. This suggests that most trails may be too challenging for young children or beginner families. More experienced families might find some moderate routes suitable, but caution is advised.
tips:
Ruins of an old water mill that marks the beginning of the Fuente del Berro River and that surrounds a curious legend.
Legend has it that it was formerly an inaccessible and forbidden place until one day a shepherd left his goats there while he hit the local pines with his stick. Suddenly the cavities of the mountain resounded, the cattle fled but the shepherd was chased by the very devil wrapped in flames. Despite this, the shepherd managed to flee by jumping into the river from the ravine, but the devil left his footprints there, which are still marked on the rock next to the mill.

It is an enormous cliff (or monolith) that rises above the Rio Blanco (Rio de la Fuente del Berro). You can get there walking from the road along a very colorful and picturesque path next to the river and on the other side the Alto de las Piedras.

We will have to leave the bicycle and walk a few meters to get closer to the beautiful Cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro in the town of Calomarde, but without a doubt it is worth it if we are in the area. Nearby there is a recreational area in case we want to rest or recover energy.

waypoints.name: Cascada del Río de la Fuente del Berro
